input.title: Self-Refine
input.article_excerpt: Self-Refine describes an iterative loop where a model generates an initial answer, produces feedback on that answer, and then refines the answer using the feedback. The key claim is that useful test-time improvement can happen without updating model weights.
input.summary_goal: Explain the mechanism and why it matters for self-improving agent workflows.

input.title: Reflexion
input.article_excerpt: Reflexion frames verbal feedback as a form of reinforcement signal. An agent stores reflection notes from failures and uses those notes to guide later attempts on related tasks.
input.summary_goal: Explain what is stored, when it is reused, and how this differs from retraining.

input.title: Rubric Drift
input.article_excerpt: A weak evaluator can make a workflow look better without actually improving output quality. If the formatter learns to satisfy labels and keywords, the score rises while the summary may stay shallow.
input.summary_goal: Explain why evaluator quality must be checked before memory experiments.
